data.title: How high can the S&P 500 climb in 2026? Here’s what prediction markets are signaling
data.description:
After briefly climbing above the 7,000 threshold earlier in 2026, the S&P 500  (SP500) has since retreated and is now hovering near the 6,836 level. The pullback has prompted investors to reassess just how far the benchmark index can advance before year-end.

Prediction market Kalshi offers a snapshot of trader expectations, outlining a range of potential upside targets. According to current probabilities, a move to 7,200 or higher carries a 66% chance, while 7,400 or above is priced at 48%. Odds start to taper with projections north of 7,600. 

More ambitious forecasts show diminishing likelihoods. Kalshi traders assign a 16% probability to 8,000 or higher, and the most bullish scenario — a rally to 9,000 or beyond — is currently priced at 6%. 

The distribution highlights cautious optimism, with traders leaning toward moderate gains rather than extreme upside. See the percentage breakdowns Kalshi data shows: 

  • 66% — 7,200 or above
  • 48% — 7,400 or above
  • 36% — 7,600 or above
  • 26% — 7,800 or above
  • 16% — 8,000 or above
  • 12% — 8,200 or above 
  • 11% — 8,400 or above
  • 7% — 8,600 or above 
  • 7% — 8,800 or above 
  • 6% — 9,000 or above 

S&P 500 ETFs: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX).

data.title: Canada’s inflation rate falls to 2.3% in January
Palo Alto Networks unveils intent to acquire Koi to boost agentic capabilities
data.description:
• Palo Alto Networks (PANW) unveiled its intent on Tuesday to acquire cybersecurity startup Koi to boost its agentic artificial intelligence prowess. 
  • Palo Alto shares dropped fractionally in premarket trading.
  • Terms of the deal were not disclosed, but Palo Alto said it would have more to say about the pending deal when it reports fiscal second-quarter results after the close of trading today.
  • "AI agents and tools are the ultimate insiders," Lee Klarich, Palo Alto's Chief Product & Technology Officer, said in a statement. "They have full access to your systems and data, but operate entirely outside the view of traditional security controls. By acquiring Koi, we will be closing this gap and setting a new standard for endpoint security. We will give our customers the visibility and control required to safely harness the power of AI—ensuring that every agent, plugin, and script is governed, verified, and secure."
  • A consensus of analysts expect Palo Alto Networks to earn $0.94 per share on $2.58B in revenue.

data.title: Ocular drops after late-stage trial data for wet AMD therapy
data.description:
Ocular Therapeutix (OCUL) lost ~22% in the premarket on Tuesday after the company released data from a late-stage trial evaluating Axpaxli, its experimental therapy for wet age-related macular degeneration, the leading cause of irreversible vision loss.

The Bedford, Massachusetts-based biopharma posted encouraging topline results from its SOL-1 Phase 3 superiority trial, which tested the tyrosine kinase inhibitor against aflibercept, a blockbuster eye disease therapy marketed by Regeneron (REGN) and Bayer (BAYZF) as Eylea.

According to the company, SOL-1 reached the superiority primary endpoint with high statistical significance, as roughly 74% of patients on Axpaxli maintained vision at Week 36 compared to about 56% of those on aflibercept, 2 mg.

The intravitreal implant was generally well tolerated with no serious ocular or systemic adverse events related to Axpaxli, Ocular (OCUL) added.

Subject to regulatory feedback, the company plans to submit a New Drug Application to the FDA for Axpaxli, which, if approved, will be the first TKI to be available in the U.S. for wet AMD.

data.title: Warner Bros. gives Paramount a week to submit final offer after rejecting latest bid
data.description:
Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) has given Paramount Skydance (PSKY) seven days to come up with their "best and final offer" and rejected the latest amended offer made by David Ellison's company.

Warner Bros. on Tuesday indicated that it expects a higher offer than $31 per share from Paramount and will begin holding discussions with them to address "deficiencies that remain unresolved and clarify certain terms."

"Following receipt of PSKY's latest amended offer, a senior representative for PSKY informed a WBD Board member that, if the WBD Board authorized discussions, PSKY would agree to pay $31 per share and that the offer was not PSKY's 'best and final' proposal," according to the press release.

"This price, along with several other matters that PSKY stated it would address in its February 10 letter, are not reflected in the latest merger agreement that PSKY proposed," WBD added.

WBD noted that Paramount's last amended offer addressed only some concerns. Ellison's latest offer notably included a "ticking fee" of up to $650M each quarter after 2026, if the regulatory approvals are delayed.

The HBO parent said it has received a limited waiver with Netflix (NFLX) until February 23 to hold discussions with Paramount. They also pointed out that Netflix retains the right to match Paramount's final offer.

Warner has set a March 20 date for a special meeting of stockholders to vote on the transaction with Netflix.

data.title: Cousins Properties sets $250M stock buyback plan
data.description:
• Cousins Properties’ (CUZ) board of directors has authorized the repurchase of up to $250M of its outstanding common shares under a newly established share repurchase program.
  • Cousins anticipates funding the plan with a combination of proceeds from non-core asset sales, retained cash, debt financing, and/or the settlement of common shares previously issued on a forward basis under Cousins' ATM program.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: ZIM, CMPS, DHR, and more
data.description:
Stock futures dipped Tuesday morning amid a holiday-shortened trading week, spotlighting key economic data and corporate earnings ahead.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • ZIM Integrated Shipping (ZIM) +36% - Shares jumped after Hapag-Lloyd (HPGLY) agreed to acquire the shipper for $35 per share in cash, a 58% premium that values the company at about $4.2B. The deal still needs approval from regulators, shareholders, and the government of Israel, which holds a strategic “golden share” in ZIM. To help secure approval, the buyer included FIMI Opportunity Funds in the transaction.
  • COMPASS Pathways (CMPS) +20% - Shares surged after the company announced it successfully met the primary endpoint in its second Phase 3 trial of COMP360 psilocybin for treatment-resistant depression, strengthening the clinical case for the therapy and boosting expectations around a potential regulatory pathway and commercialization prospects.
  • Fiserv (FISV) +4% - Shares rose following a report that activist Jana Partners has built a stake and is pushing for steps to lift the stock, including accelerating growth in the core banking franchise and reviewing non-strategic assets. The investor is said to support the firm’s “One Fiserv” strategy and CEO Mike Lyons. Jana is not expected to seek a split of the payments and fintech businesses. The size of the stake and any board ambitions remain unclear.

Biggest stock losers

  • Ocular Therapeutix (OCUL) -27% - Shares plunged even after reporting positive topline results from its SOL-1 Phase 3 superiority trial of AXPAXLI for wet AMD, as investors appeared to focus on uncertainty around commercialization timing, durability of benefit, and next regulatory steps despite the study meeting its primary endpoint.
  • Danaher (DHR) -7% - Shares dipped amid reports it is nearing a ~$10B deal to acquire medical technology firm Masimo (MASI), a price implying a significant premium to its roughly $7B market value, while Masimo (MASI) shares jumped 34% on the news. The potential acquisition will be Danaher’s largest since buying Cytiva. It would expand its life sciences and diagnostics footprint, with Masimo known for pulse oximetry devices and its ongoing patent dispute with Apple over the Apple Watch.
  • Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM) -3% - Shares slipped after the company agreed to acquire BHP’s (BHP) 33.75% silver stream at the Antamina mine for $4.3B in cash, lifting its total share to 67.5%. The deal, effective April 2026, is expected to add ~6 Moz of annual silver output in the first five years, raise 2026 production by ~11%, and expand reserves. The deal will be funded through cash, debt, and credit facilities, leaving net debt near $2.4B at closing.

More on related stocks:

  • ZIM Integrated: Hapag-Lloyd Offer Makes Sense For Everyone, Will It Go Through?
  • Ocular Therapeutix: Considering Their Phase 3 'Superiority' Trial's Prospect
  • ZIM Integrated Shipping: Why The Buyout Matters Most - Initiating Strong Buy
  • Hapag-Lloyd to buy ZIM Integrated Shipping in $4.2B deal
  • FDA push to fast-track Compass Pathways’ psychedelic treatment rejected by Trump officials, STAT News reports

data.title: Franklin Electric misses top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Franklin Electric press release (FELE): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.87 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $506.9M (+4.4% Y/Y) misses by $9.35M.
  • Net sales increased across all three segments; 4% in Water Systems, 9% in Energy Systems, and 3% in Distribution
  • Operating income was $51.6 million, an increase of 20% to the prior year, with operating margin of 10.2%.

  • Cash Flow

    The Company ended 2025 with a cash balance of $99.7 million, a decrease of $120.9 million compared to the end of 2024. Net cash flows from operating activities for 2025 were $238.9 million versus $261.4 million in the same period in 2024.

    2026 Guidance

    The Company expects its guidance for full year 2026 sales to be in the range of $2.17 billion to $2.24 billion vs. $2.24B consensus and full year 2026 Adjusted Diluted EPS to be in the range of $4.40 to $4.60 vs. $4.73 consensus.

data.title: Compass gains as psychedelic therapy succeeds in late-stage trial for depression
data.description:
Compass Pathways (CMPS) added ~20% in the premarket on Tuesday after the U.K.-based biotech announced that its psychedelic therapy COMP360 reached the primary endpoint in a late-stage trial for patients with depression.

According to the company, COMP006, the second of two pivotal Phase 3 trials for COMP360, reached the primary endpoint related to the difference from baseline in the Montgomery-Åsberg Depression Rating Scale ((MADRS)) at week six.

The randomized study of 581 patients, running alongside its sister study COMP005, was designed to evaluate the effect of two fixed doses of COMP360 in those with treatment-resistant depression.

Detailed results indicated that in Part A of the study, designed to test two doses of COMP360 administered three weeks apart, reduced the symptom severity, leading to a -3.8-point statistically significant difference in MADRS at the primary endpoint.

Regarding safety, the company stated that the psilocybin-based therapy was generally well-tolerated, and its treatment-emergent adverse events were mild or moderate in severity.

Compass (CMPS) has requested a meeting with the FDA to discuss the results and pursue a rolling submission for a market application, expected to be filed in Q4.
